screens.name: Explicit run targeting contract
screens.purpose: Ensure supervisor commands act only on a named run and never on hidden global state.
screens.primary_action: Invoke a supervisor command with a required run id

ux_rules: Prefer explicit run selection over convenience shortcuts when ambiguity could corrupt workflow state.
Make missing-run errors actionable by showing the exact expected --run-id usage.
Keep init independent from later command execution so run creation and run selection stay separate concerns.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Represent supervisor targeting as explicit command input rather than global repo state.
interaction_risks: A missing run id may still lead to ambiguous usage if error messaging is weak.
Documentation may lag behind the CLI contract and cause user confusion.
notes: This run targets supervisor protocol behavior, not a user-facing product UI.

constraints: Do not keep any fallback path that resolves an existing run without an explicit run id.
Update documentation and agent instructions in the same change as the CLI contract.
Any repo-local action wrapper that targets workflow state must require --run-id and verify that the target run directory exists.
open_decisions: Whether future helper actions should also require run ids positionally or via named flags remains open.

critical_judgments: The new contract should eliminate repo-global run ambiguity.
The CLI errors should remain clear and copy-pasteable.
